sense.definition: a lightweight object used in badminton, hit back and forth over the net

examples.x: She served the shuttlecock with precision, aiming for the corner of the court.
He practiced his overhead smash to send the shuttlecock across the net.
The shuttlecock floated gracefully through the air during the rally.
The shuttlecock's feathers were carefully arranged for optimal flight.
They replaced the shuttlecock regularly to maintain consistent play.

sense.definition: a solid rubber ball used in the sport of lacrosse

examples.x: She caught the fast-moving lacrosse ball with a quick flick of her wrist.
The lacrosse ball bounced off the goalpost after a powerful shot.
He cradled the lacrosse ball in the pocket of his stick while running down the field.

sense.definition: the metal ball that athletes throw for distance in shot put competitions

examples.x: He positioned the shot against his neck before his throw.
The shot slipped from his grip during the spin technique.
He balanced the shot in his hand, focusing on his throwing stance.
She practiced her shot put technique with a heavier shot for strength training.
They lined up to take turns throwing the shot in the competition.

sense.definition: a round object that is used in games and sports, such as soccer, basketball, bowling, etc.

examples.x: I hit the ball with a golf club, sending it flying towards the hole.
I threw the ball to my friend during a game of catch.
She caught the ball with her glove during the baseball game.
We played a game of tennis using a yellow ball.
We watched a game of volleyball and saw the players spike the ball.

sense.definition: a small, dimpled ball used in the sport of golf, designed to be hit with various clubs towards a hole on a course

examples.x: The golfer selected a new golf ball from his bag for the next hole.
His golf ball bounced off the tree and landed in the rough.
She marked her golf ball on the green before lining up her putt.
She practiced her chip shots with different types of golf balls.
She collected several golf balls from the water hazard during the round.

sense.definition: a ball used in the sport of baseball, typically made of leather

examples.x: He threw the baseball with precision, aiming for the catcher's mitt.
His favorite baseball had signatures from his favorite players.
The umpire checked the baseballs regularly for game readiness.
They used special baseballs for batting practice to conserve game balls.

sense.definition: a round ball used in the sport of basketball, typically made of leather or synthetic material

examples.x: He dribbled the basketball down the court with skill and speed.
She practiced shooting free throws with the basketball after practice.
He spun the basketball on his finger during halftime entertainment.
The basketball bounced off the rim and into the hands of a teammate.
He admired the autographed basketball displayed in his office.

sense.definition: the ball used in handball, typically made of leather or synthetic material

examples.x: He dribbled the handball skillfully down the court.
She threw the handball into the top corner of the net for a goal.
His team practiced passing drills with the handball during training.
She caught the fast-moving handball with a quick reaction.
The referee inspected the handball for proper inflation before the match.

sense.definition: a hard, leather-covered ball used in the sport of cricket

examples.x: The bowler polished the cricket ball to enhance its shine.
She defended her wicket against the spinning cricket ball.
His cricket ball swung late, deceiving the batsman.
He bowled a fast delivery with the cricket ball, surprising the batsman.
She hit the cricket ball cleanly for a boundary.

sense.definition: a small, hollow ball covered in felt, used in tennis

examples.x: She served the tennis ball with speed and accuracy.
He practiced his forehand strokes with the tennis ball.
The coach demonstrated the proper grip for holding the tennis ball.
Her tennis ball bounced twice before reaching the opponent's side.
They retrieved stray tennis balls from the surrounding courts.

sense.definition: a heavy ball used in the sport of bowling, typically weighing between 10 and 16 pounds, with a diameter of approximately 8.5 inches

examples.x: I forgot to bring my bowling ball to the alley, so I had to rent one.
The bowling ball felt heavy in my hands, but I managed to roll a strike.
My friends and I spent the afternoon at the bowling alley, each bringing our favorite bowling ball.
After practicing for weeks, I finally got comfortable with a heavier bowling ball.

sense.definition: a round ball used in volleyball, usually made of leather or synthetic material

examples.x: He served the volleyball with precision, aiming for the back corner.
Her dig saved the volleyball from hitting the ground.
The coach taught them the fundamentals of passing the volleyball.
She bumped the volleyball to keep the rally going.
He practiced his jump serve technique with the volleyball.

sense.definition: a round object used in cue sports like pool, snooker, and carom billiards, often made of phenolic resin or other materials

examples.x: The billiard ball rolled smoothly into the corner pocket.
His shot banked off two billiard balls before sinking the target ball.
He calculated the angle needed to hit the billiard ball into the side pocket.
She replaced the worn billiard balls with new ones for better gameplay.
He aimed carefully and struck the billiard ball with the cue stick.

sense.definition: an oval-shaped ball used in rugby, typically made of leather or synthetic material

examples.x: She caught the rugby ball and ran towards the try line.
His drop kick with the rugby ball sailed between the posts.
The referee tossed the rugby ball into the scrum to restart play.
He punted the rugby ball downfield to gain territorial advantage.
The player passed the rugby ball to his teammate before being tackled.

sense.definition: a small, rubber ball used in the sport of squash, designed to be hit against the walls of the court with a squash racket

examples.x: She hit the squash ball with precision, aiming for the front wall.
Her squash ball bounced twice before reaching the front wall.
He practiced his drop shots with a new squash ball.
The coach demonstrated the proper grip for holding the squash ball.
He retrieved the squash ball from the back corner after a tight rally.

sense.definition: a small rubber ball with a high bounce, specifically designed for use in the racquetball sport

examples.x: He served the racquetball into the corner with precision.
His lob shot with the racquetball landed deep in the opponent's court.
She returned the racquetball with a powerful backhand swing.
They measured the speed of the racquetball serves during practice.
He aimed his shot with the racquetball to hit the side wall and confuse his opponent.

sense.definition: a hard, durable ball used in the sport of polo, typically made of plastic or wood

examples.x: He struck the polo ball with his mallet, sending it down the field.
She passed the polo ball to her teammate, who scored a goal.
She dribbled the polo ball expertly, maneuvering past defenders.
They practiced their swing techniques with the polo ball during training.
His powerful shot with the polo ball sailed over the opponent's defense.

sense.definition: a round ball designed for players to kick and maneuver during the game of football
a hard, spherical object made of resin or wood used in a sport called bocce

examples.x: He rolled the bocce ball gently toward the pallino, aiming to get closer than his opponent.
The players measured the distance of each bocce ball from the pallino to determine the winner.
The referee marked the spot where the bocce ball stopped, ensuring fair play.
She tossed the bocce ball underhand, trying to place it strategically near the pallino.
His bocce ball knocked the opponent's ball away from the pallino, changing the game's momentum.

sense.definition: a football, typically made from leather

examples.x: He threw the pigskin across the field.
The quarterback tightly gripped the pigskin before the snap.
The coach instructed the team to practice handling the pigskin more securely.
Players often protect the pigskin with their bodies during tackles.
The pigskin became slippery in the rain, affecting the players' grip.

sense.definition: a small, lightweight ball used in the sport of table tennis

examples.x: The table tennis ball bounced off the edge of the table.
She served the table tennis ball with precision.
He carried a spare table tennis ball in his pocket during matches.
The old table tennis ball lost its bounce and needed replacing.
She kept a spare table tennis ball in her pocket for quick access.

sense.definition: a brightly colored, gripped ball used in the fast-paced water sport

examples.x: The twins argued over who got to use the brand new water polo ball.
The goalkeeper struggled to catch the slippery water polo ball.
I practiced throwing the water polo ball against the pool wall.
The coach tossed the water polo ball in the air, signaling the start of the drill.
The crowd cheered as the player launched the water polo ball into the net.

sense.definition: a light, plastic ball with holes, used in the indoor game

examples.x: The bright colors of the floorball ball make it easy to see on the court.
The referee stopped the game to replace a worn-out floorball ball.
Compared to a hockey puck, a floorball ball is much lighter and easier to control.
Make sure to inflate the floorball ball to the recommended pressure for optimal play.
During practice, we focused on drills to improve our passing accuracy with the floorball ball.

sense.definition: a rubber ball similar to a soccer ball, used in the mixed-gender sport

examples.x: The attacker jumped high to shoot the korfball ball through the korf.
Don't forget to bring a spare korfball ball in case the main one gets damaged.
Unlike basketball, a korfball ball can't be dribbled for long distances.
They practiced throwing the korfball ball from different angles for better accuracy.
The korfball ball needs to be inflated properly to ensure a good bounce.

sense.definition: the small target ball that is aimed for in bocce

examples.x: The team strategically placed the pallino ball near the sidewall.
I focused on throwing my bocce ball closer to the pallino ball than my opponent.
After several throws, my teammate's bocce ball finally nudged the pallino ball.
It can be tricky to throw the pallino ball far down the court without it rolling off.
Beginners often struggle to judge the distance when throwing the pallino ball.

sense.definition: a small, woven ball made of rattan or synthetic material

examples.x: Keeping the sepak takraw ball off the ground is the main objective of the game.
A well-made sepak takraw ball should be lightweight and have a good bounce.
The bright colors on the sepak takraw ball make it easy to follow during matches.

sense.definition: a small, bouncy ball used in a team sport similar to volleyball, but played with fists

examples.x: Unlike volleyball, players can only hit the fistball with their closed fists.
A well-placed fistball can be difficult for the opposing team to dig.
The fistball soared over the net as the attacker spiked it.
The loud thump of the fistball hitting the court echoed through the gym.
We need a new fistball – the old one is starting to crack.

sense.definition: the hard, stitched ball used in hurling and similar Gaelic games

examples.x: The hurler skillfully maneuvered the sliotar down the pitch.
A well-placed shot with the sliotar can soar a great distance.
The sliotar grazed the goalkeeper's fingertips before flying into the net.
Players can carry the sliotar for a maximum of four steps.
Beginners in hurling practice basic skills like striking the sliotar on the ground.

sense.definition: a hard rubber ball, similar in size to a tennis ball, used in the winter sport of bandy

examples.x: Unlike hockey pucks, bandy balls can be lifted and controlled with the stick.
Beginners in bandy often struggle with keeping their eye on the fast-moving bandy ball.
Bandy balls are typically orange or cerise in color for better visibility on the ice.
Remember to check the inflation of the bandy ball before each game.
Passing the bandy ball quickly and accurately is a key skill in bandy.

sense.definition: a plastic disc used in various recreational activities and sports, such as ultimate frisbee and disc golf

examples.x: He tossed the flying disc across the park to his friend.
His dog eagerly chased after the flying disc in the open field.
The ultimate frisbee team trained with flying discs during practice.
He threw the flying disc with a sidearm flick for better distance.
The flying disc glided smoothly through the air before being caught.

sense.definition: a small, flat rubber disk used in ice hockey

examples.x: She skillfully passed the puck to her teammate for the winning goal.
The goalie made an impressive save by stopping the puck with his glove.
The puck slid across the ice, barely missing the goal.
The team practiced controlling the puck in tight spaces.
The referee dropped the puck to start the game.
